headline: China market for Nvidia AI chips to open 'over time': Huang
summary: Nvidia boss Jensen Huang expects China to eventually open its market to high-end US chips that can train and run artificial intelligence systems."My sense is that over time the market will open," added Huang, CEO of Nvidia -- the world's most valuable company, due to huge demand for its AI hardware.

content.title: China market for Nvidia AI chips to open 'over time': Huang
content.summary: Nvidia boss Jensen Huang expects China to eventually open its market to high-end US chips that can train and run artificial intelligence systems."My sense is that over time the market will open," added Huang, CEO of Nvidia -- the world's most valuable company, due to huge demand for its AI hardware.

headline: NVIDIA Extends On Premises AI Reach As Valuation Stays Above Fair Value
summary: Dell Technologies and NVIDIA (NasdaqGS:NVDA) launched Dell Deskside Agentic AI, available immediately. The solution lets enterprises run and manage agentic AI models from 30 billion to 1 trillion parameters locally on high-performance workstations. It extends the Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA across desktops and datacenter servers using NVIDIA OpenShell and NemoClaw. The platform focuses on data sovereignty, predictable costs, and secure scaling for multi-agent AI workflows. For investors...

headline: Chinese Blockade Risks Put TSMC’s Central Chip Role Under Scrutiny
summary: Rising geopolitical tensions with China have sharpened focus on the risk of a potential blockade of Taiwan. Such a move could severely disrupt Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing’s chip production and shipments for key clients including Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, and Apple. Investors are assessing how a prolonged disruption could affect global supply chains in technology, autos, and energy, while possibly shifting demand toward non Taiwanese chipmakers and shipping companies. Taiwan...

headline: Seagate Slips as CEO Says New Factories ‘Take Too Long’
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Seagate Technology Holdings shares fell 6.9% Monday in its worst one-day drop in nearly two months after management comments at a JPMorgan conference sparked investor fears that the company won’t be able to keep up with soaring demand for memory chips.
